title: Digital Transformation Roadmap 2026
body: Organization-wide transformation program led by Executive Steering Committee. Five pillars driving change across all business units. Q1 2026 kickoff.

title: Why Change Now: The Case for Transformation
body: Revenue growth stalled at 3% vs. industry 12%. Legacy systems causing $4.2M annual inefficiency. Customer NPS dropped from 67 to 44. Board mandated digital acceleration by end of 2026. Key drivers: competitor pressure, regulatory changes, talent attrition.

title: Current State Assessment
body: Strengths: Strong brand (78% recognition), loyal customer base (15-year avg relationship). Weaknesses: Siloed data (7 separate systems), manual processes (40% of tasks), outdated CRM (2014). Opportunities: AI automation ($2M potential savings), cloud migration (scalability), open banking API. Threats: FinTech disruptors (3 new entrants), talent war (22% tech turnover), regulatory complexity.

title: Five Core Strategic Pillars
body: 1. Digitise Core: Automate top 20 manual workflows. 2. Data First: Unified data platform — single source of truth. 3. Customer 360: Real-time personalisation engine. 4. Talent Uplift: Digital upskilling for 800 employees. 5. Ecosystem Play: API partnerships with 12 fintechs. Each pillar has dedicated workstream lead and budget allocation.

title: Section 01 — Digital Core
body: Automating core operations to reduce cost and increase speed. Target: 40% reduction in processing time by Q3.

title: Headline KPI: $24M ARR ↑117% YoY
body: Annual Recurring Revenue reached $24M, up 117% year-over-year. Key driver: enterprise segment expansion (+43 new logos). Net Revenue Retention: 128%. CAC payback period: 11 months.

title: Tier Comparison: Current vs Target vs Future State
body: Comparing our current capabilities, 12-month target state, and 3-year future state across key dimensions: Process Automation, Real-time Data, Customer Self-Service, AI Decision Support, API Ecosystem, Cloud-native Architecture.

title: Change Journey & Risk Mitigation
body: Phase 1 (Q1): Mobilise — governance, quick wins. Phase 2 (Q2-Q3): Transform — core system migration, process redesign. Phase 3 (Q4): Sustain — embed new ways of working, measure outcomes. Top risks: Change fatigue (HIGH), data migration errors (MEDIUM), vendor delays (LOW).

title: Programme Timeline 2026
body: January: Programme launch + steering committee. March: Core systems design complete. June: Wave 1 go-live (Finance + HR). September: Wave 2 go-live (Operations + Customer). December: Full programme completion + retrospective. Benefits realisation review Q1 2027.
